description: 40 - 31-12-2024 - Emails between Sir Philip Barton (former Permanent Under-Secretary, FCDO) and Robert Tinline (Americas Director, FCDO)
text:
FCDO) and Robert Tinline (Americas Director, FCDO)

From: Philip R Barton[/ Personal
Sent: Tue 31/12/2024 11:50:51 AM (UTC)
To: JCS Personal
Subject: RE: HMA-Des Engagement
Thanks
| am content for Peter to be given IT on the official tier in advance of him starting formally on
13 January.
Philip
From: JCS

Sent: 31 December 2024 10:19
To: Philip R Barton
Subject: FW: HMA-Des Engagement
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - FCDO ONLY

Hello — do you have a view on whether Peter’ s IT can issue before 13 Jan? Are you
happy to agree a waiver so it can be issued before his contract starts and vetting is
completed?Thank you!

From: JCS
Sent: 31 December 2024 10:17
To: Robert Tinline; JCS

Ce: JCS ¢ JCS 7 JCS
Subject: RE: HMA-Des Engagement
Hi Rob,

Given Peter has been announced, his on boarding is necessarily taking place in
parallel to him wrapping up his affairs.
In terms of what is appropriate before he joins FCDO payroll — I'll ask Philip for a
waiver to issue his IT prior to his contract starting and clearances being granted. On
introductory calls, we 13 January looks like it will be his first formal week in the role
but fine if he is willing to make time to see people before then on an informal basis.
Thanks,
Jcs
From: Robert Tinline
Sent: 31 December 2024 09:32
To: JCS ;
Ce: JCS; JCS ; JCS
Subject: RE: HMA-Des Engagement

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - FCDO ONLY
Jcs
Thanks, clear. Is that therefore a decision that Peter starts employment with HMG on 13
January?
Does that mean Philip wants us to pause intro calls, induction into IT ete until 13 January? Or
does he wish to proceed with that from 6 January?

126

Rob
Rob Tinline | Americas Director | Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office
 | 
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - FCDO ONLY
From: 
Sent: 31 December 2024 09:25
To: 
 
Cc: Robert Tinline ; 
 ; 
 ; 
Subject: RE: HMA-Des Engagement
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - FCDO ONLY
Dear 
I’ve now spoken with Philip. His view was as follows:
• LPM has been announced publicly as the next HMA Washington as a political
appointee. The appointment is subject to agrement and security clearance:
• Prior to taking up this employment, LPM plans to participate in an international
event relating to China. This is renumerated;
• As he will not by then have taken up employment with HMG, it is a matter for
LPM whether or not he proceeds with this event.
He has asked that any concerns are flagged to SpAds so that they and No 10 can 
take a view as to whether they wish to intervene with LPM.
Please could you share a very short summary of views 
(BEW/China/Americas/HRD/Security) and I’ll take this forward?
Thank you,
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - FCDO ONLY
From: 
Sent: 30 December 2024 11:46
To: 
Cc: Robert Tinline ; 
 ; 
 ; 
Subject: HMA-Des Engagement
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - FCDO ONLY
,
You requested some lines on what we know about HMA-des’s proposed engagement. Please 
see below. And I’ll message you separately with the informal view we’ve received from BEW 
and China Desk.
• HMA-Des has committed to participating in a panel event at investment bank UBS’s
Greater China Conference, hosted in Shanghai from 11-13 January. This is a major
event with ~4000 attendees and considerable media. Many sessions are recorded. See
some high-level detail here and a summary of last year’s conference here.
• We understand he would plan to attend virtually and participate in his Global Counsel
capacity. We also understand that he would be paid for this engagement.
• HMA-Des has asked to start on FCDO payroll from w/c 13 January in order to
facilitate this private engagement.
• We will need to inform him of our decision or advice ASAP this week so he can inform
the organisers in good time.
Personal
Personal
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
127


We don’t have more detail on the precise nature/topic of his panel.
You will kindly test with SpAds. Is Philip’s view that this is ultimately for No10 to decide given 
the political considerations? We/Rob stand ready to offer geographic advice if requested. I’m 
copying 
 who is looped in on this and can give a view from HRD/security.
Thanks,
 | Head of US Global Issues and Canada Team | US and Canada 
Department | Americas and SIDS Directorate | Foreign, Commonwealth & 
Development Office | E: 
 | M: 
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - FCDO ONLY
Personal
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
128
parsing_notes: Extract structured email headers (From, To, Cc, Subject, Date/Sent) for each message in the chain. Identify reply/forward nesting depth. Parse each message body separately. Redacted addresses appear as 'PERSONAL' — names are retained. Build sender→recipient edges per message for communication graph.
